name: Fix hybrid_search defaults + cardinality routing
description: M2 + premortem M2 amendment. Fix hybrid_search() default from specter2 to indus. Skip OpenAI signal entirely (0 rows). Add cardinality-aware query routing: estimate filter selectivity before HNSW scan, use filter-first CTE + brute-force cosine for <1% selectivity filters. This is all in search.py — no mcp_server.py changes.
acceptance: grep -n 'specter2' src/scix/search.py returns zero hits in default parameter values
hybrid_search() accepts model_name='indus' as default and produces results via rrf_fuse()
When filters have <1% selectivity, hybrid_search uses filter-first CTE instead of HNSW iterative scan
OpenAI embedding signal is skipped (not called) when model has 0 rows in paper_embeddings
Existing tests in tests/ pass (pytest tests/ -x)
New test: test_hybrid_search_default_model verifies indus is default
New test: test_cardinality_routing verifies filter-first fallback triggers for selective filters

name: Pin MCP SDK + vendor UAT SKOS
description: M10: Pin mcp SDK to >=1.2,<2.0 in requirements. Fix UAT SKOS URL — either vendor the file locally or pin to a release tag instead of master branch. Add startup self-test that validates list_tools() returns expected tool count with valid schemas.
acceptance: requirements.txt (or pyproject.toml) pins mcp to '>=1.2,<2.0' or equivalent upper bound
UAT SKOS URL no longer references 'master' branch — uses release tag, 'main', or local vendored file
grep -rn 'master' src/scix/uat.py returns zero hits for GitHub raw URLs
Existing tests pass (pytest tests/ -x)

name: Consolidate 28 MCP tools to 13 + aliases + implicit session
description: M1+M3+M4+M5+M6+M8: The big refactor of mcp_server.py. (1) Replace semantic_search+keyword_search with unified 'search' tool backed by hybrid_search (mode=hybrid/semantic/keyword). (2) Merge get_citations+get_references into citation_graph(direction=forward/backward/both), merge co_citation+bibliographic_coupling into citation_similarity(method=...), fold get_citation_context into citation_graph(include_context=true). (3) Merge entity_search+resolve_entity into entity(action=search/resolve), keep entity_context separate. Fix entity_type validation. (4) Eliminate add_to_working_set, get_working_set, get_session_summary, clear_working_set — replace with implicit tracking in SessionState. Add clear_first param to find_gaps. (5) Merge get_paper+document_context via include_entities flag (default false per premortem). Absorb get_openalex_topics, entity_profile. (6) Add deprecated alias dict in _dispatch_tool — old names removed from list_tools() but still dispatched with deprecation metadata. Log alias hits. list_tools() returns exactly 13 tools.
acceptance: list_tools() returns exactly 13 tools: search, concept_search, get_paper, read_paper, citation_graph, citation_similarity, citation_chain, entity, entity_context, graph_context, find_gaps, temporal_evolution, facet_counts
search(query='test', mode='hybrid') dispatches to hybrid_search and returns results
search(query='test', mode='semantic') dispatches to vector_search with indus model
search(query='test', mode='keyword') dispatches to lexical_search
citation_graph(bibcode='...', direction='forward') returns same shape as old get_citations
citation_graph(bibcode='...', direction='backward') returns same shape as old get_references
citation_similarity(bibcode='...', method='co_citation') returns same shape as old co_citation_analysis
entity(action='search', entity_type='methods', query='JWST') returns papers
entity(action='search', entity_type='entities') returns clear validation error listing valid types
entity(action='resolve', query='James Webb') returns entity candidates
entity_context(entity_id=1) returns entity card (or appropriate error for nonexistent ID)
get_paper(bibcode='...', include_entities=false) returns paper metadata only
get_paper(bibcode='...', include_entities=true) returns paper + linked entities from agent_document_context view
find_gaps() reads from implicit session state without requiring add_to_working_set calls
find_gaps(clear_first=true) resets the focused set before computing gaps
add_to_working_set, get_working_set, get_session_summary, clear_working_set are NOT in list_tools()
Calling old tool name 'semantic_search' returns results + deprecated:true + use_instead:'search' in metadata
Calling old tool name 'get_citations' returns results + deprecated:true + use_instead:'citation_graph'
All deprecated aliases log the original tool name via _log_query or similar
Existing passing tests still pass (pytest tests/ -x --ignore tests that test removed tools)
New tests verify at least 3 consolidated tools dispatch correctly

name: Rewrite all 13 tool descriptions (intent-action-contrast)
description: M7: Rewrite every tool description in list_tools() using intent-action-contrast pattern. Each description: what does the agent get, when to use vs alternatives, what NOT to use it for. No implementation details (no pgvector, HNSW, tsvector, JSONB, GIN, model identifiers). Under 80 words. Include one 'Use X instead when...' contrast sentence.
acceptance: All 13 tool descriptions are under 80 words each
No tool description contains any of: pgvector, HNSW, tsvector, JSONB, GIN, cosine, vector, embedding model names
Every tool description includes a 'Use X instead when...' or equivalent contrast sentence
Descriptions follow intent-action-contrast pattern: what agent gets, when to use, when NOT to use
grep -i 'pgvector\|hnsw\|tsvector\|jsonb\|gin\|specter\|indus\|text-embedding' across all descriptions returns zero hits
Existing tests pass (pytest tests/ -x)

name: All-tool smoke test + startup self-test
description: M10 (test part): Add a startup self-test in mcp_server.py that validates list_tools() returns 13 tools with valid schemas on server init. Add a pytest smoke test that calls each of the 13 consolidated tools with a golden-path input and verifies non-error response. This catches total breakage of any tool on every deploy.
acceptance: A startup self-test runs during MCP server initialization and validates list_tools() returns exactly 13 tools
If startup self-test fails, server logs a CRITICAL error (does not silently start with broken tools)
pytest test file exists that calls each of the 13 tools with a minimal valid input
Smoke test verifies each tool returns a non-error response (no exceptions, no 'error' key in result)
pytest tests/test_smoke.py (or similar) passes
Existing tests still pass (pytest tests/ -x)
